extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ crashed twice in the opening 30km, lost over 2 minutes chasing solo, SCHACHMANN took GC by 52 seconds from VLASOV while CORT won the sprint finish.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ used BENNETT and KRAGH ANDERSEN as breakaway insurance before crashes, revealing team sacrificed stage ambitions for GC buffer.
SCHACHMANN's team buried themselves after 25km crashes, recognizing crosswind descent section would prevent ROGLIČ's teammates from pacing back effectively.
DECLERCQ appeared late in race attempting to pace ROGLIČ back, suggesting Jumbo-Visma called rival teams for help in desperation.
MÄDER's near-stage win on previous mountain stage showed Bahrain willing to exhaust young GC talent for stage hunting over placement.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: two crashes within 30km on descent/crosswind sections exposed isolation vulnerability when teammates already spent from breakaway duties
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: positive: team recognized tactical opportunity immediately after first ROGLIČ crash and committed fully to tempo rather than waiting
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: rode breakaway in green jersey to help CATTANEO and secure points, suggesting sprinters willingly sacrifice for team GC
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: fastest sprinter in reduced bunch finish, reliably converts reduced field opportunities into wins
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: finished 23 seconds behind SCHACHMANN for second overall, quietly consistent without single flashy performance
rider_signals.IZAGIRRE Ion: positive: third overall shows Astana depth when protected throughout rather than working for single leader
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: neutral: caught by ROGLIČ at 25m on previous stage despite leading to line, lacks finishing punch against world-class riders
rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: negative: exhausted helping ROGLIČ after first crash, unavailable when second crash required immediate high-tempo chase
rider_signals.BENNETT George: negative: used completely to pace ROGLIČ back after crashes, left nothing for teammate support in final kilometers
rider_signals.DECLERCQ Tim: neutral: attempted late pacing for rival ROGLIČ suggesting cross-team desperation aid, nearly caused third crash on twisty roads
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: second in stage sprint shows climbing resilience for non-pure climber in reduced field
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: positive: third in stage sprint indicates improved positioning skills in chaotic finale
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: negative: crashed separately from ROGLIČ incidents, suggesting descent/handling issues were course-wide not rider-specific
